summary: Tested Collection Detail Page Settings feature. Backend API: 12/12 tests passed. Frontend admin panel had a critical bug (TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined reading 'map') which was FIXED by implementing deep merge for settings and adding null checks. After fix, admin panel loads correctly with all settings sections. Public collection page correctly shows/hides UI elements based on admin settings.

minor.issue: Endpoint does not require authentication - potential security concern

bugs_fixed.issue: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'map') when loading settings
bugs_fixed.root_cause: Shallow merge in fetchSettings() was overwriting nested objects, losing arrays like 'badges' and 'accessories'
bugs_fixed.fix_applied: 1) Implemented deepMerge helper function for proper nested object merging. 2) Added null checks with || [] for .map() calls on badges and accessories arrays

Settings persist and can be retrieved

critical_code_review_comments: POST endpoint for collection-detail-settings does not require authentication - consider adding auth requirement for security
Frequently Bought Together section uses MOCKED/hardcoded accessory data - this is known and expected per requirements
context_for_next_testing_agent: Collection Detail Page Settings feature is fully functional. Admin panel at /admin/collection-detail-settings allows toggling Trust Badges, Delivery Estimate, Share Buttons, Accordion Sections, Frequently Bought Together, and Sticky Mobile Cart. Settings are fetched from GET /api/website-admin/collection-detail-settings (public, no auth) and saved via POST (also no auth currently). Public collection pages at /shop/collection/{series} respect these settings. A bug was fixed in CollectionDetailSettings.jsx where shallow merge was causing TypeError - now uses deep merge.

dashboard_historical_entries_500.description: Dashboard shows 500 error for historical entries API
dashboard_historical_entries_500.impact: Low - does not affect the tested feature
dashboard_historical_entries_500.note: Pre-existing issue, not related to Collection Detail Settings feature
